sourceMovie.title: The Omen
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Omen (1976) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Richard Donner's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: From Dusk Till Dawn
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Omen clicked for you, From Dusk Till Dawn (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Omen?
faq.answer: Try From Dusk Till Dawn, Orphan, The Pope's Exorcist—each shares DNA with The Omen while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Omen?
faq.answer: The Omen (1976) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Omen part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Omen Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
